sections.title: Fats
sections.narration: 22.3g of fat. saturated fat, omega3, and polyunsaturated fat are doing most of the work. for seeds, the fat profile is one of the biggest reasons they earn their place.
sections.subtitleText: 22.3g of fat. saturated fat, omega3, and polyunsaturated fat are doing most of the work. for seeds, the fat profile is one of the biggest reasons they earn their place.

sections.title: Carbs
sections.narration: 44.2g of carbs. starch is doing most of the heavy lifting here. for a seed, this is a very strong sign.

sections.title: Proteins
sections.narration: 16.7g of protein. bioavailability and amino acid quality are both strong. protein is a bonus, but not enough by itself.
sections.subtitleText: 16.7g of protein. bioavailability and amino acid quality are both strong. protein is a bonus, but not enough by itself.

sections.title: Vitamins
sections.narration: vitamin e at 18% daily value stands out most. that is barely moving the needle.

sections.title: Minerals
sections.narration: magnesium at 92% daily value, iron at 55% daily value, calcium at 30% daily value stand out most. that gives this food real mineral support.
sections.subtitleText: magnesium at 92% daily value, iron at 55% daily value, calcium at 30% daily value stand out most. that gives this food real mineral support.

sections.title: Pros
sections.narration: on the plus side: usually brings decent fat support because that is one of the category strengths. often brings dense micronutrient support because that is part of what makes the category interesting. easy to sprinkle into meals because that practical versatility matters.

sections.title: Cons
sections.narration: the drawbacks next: paper scores can overstate realistic serving sizes because people often eat far less than 100 grams. real serving sizes are often small because some strong paper numbers are hard to cash out in practice. texture or prep can be a barrier because not everyone uses seeds consistently.

closing.summary: Nigella Seeds does have some solid upside. but the tradeoffs keep it from climbing higher. best as a support food that boosts meals instead of carrying them alone.

closing.cta: Would you rank it the same, or nah?
